description_long: This object consists of three large rectangular solar panels mounted on a patterned, flat base that resembles a stylized rooftop or platform. The solar panels have a grid of dark photovoltaic cells framed by metallic borders, and each panel is slightly elevated above the base, supported by visible mounting structures. The panels are arranged in a staggered, parallel configuration, with two panels aligned closely and the third offset to the side. The base features a geometric, striped pattern in shades of gray and black, suggesting a modern or architectural context. No visible electrical cabling or inverter components are present on the exterior. The overall design is sleek and utilitarian, suitable for urban solar energy generation on rooftops or dedicated platforms.
description: A set of three large, grid-patterned solar panels mounted on a geometric, striped base, arranged in a staggered parallel layout. The panels are elevated and framed, with a modern, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing three solar panels elevated on a patterned base, with two panels close together and one offset to the right.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the staggered arrangement and elevation of the panels above the base.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the rectangular layout of the panels and the full geometric pattern of the base.
description_view_3: Rear angled view emphasizing the mounting structure and the offset positioning of the third panel.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with visible metallic support frames, likely aluminum or steel
attribute_array_configuration: three panels in a staggered, parallel arrangement on a single base
